characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Eyepatch of Power: And a medical one at that, when Tom becomes aInspector Javerttype and aggressively pursueshis wife's murderers. Kick the Dog: Tom reaches this during his darker moments after the station bombing and his wife's murder, in particular when he tries to drown Tarni Baxter. Lampshade Hanging: Tom wanted a review team to think Mount Thomas was the crime capital of Australia. Which it pretty much is.Later he would tell a French film maker that a missing police member is not a subject for television. This was about the fourth time this plot was used that year. Later he would tell a French film maker that a missing police member is not a subject for television. This was about the fourth time this plot was used that year. Punctuated!  For!  Emphasis!: "You shot. Doherty's. Dog?!"

Lampshade Hanging: Tom wanted a review team to think Mount Thomas was the crime capital of Australia. Which it pretty much is.Later he would tell a French film maker that a missing police member is not a subject for television. This was about the fourth time this plot was used that year. characters.name: P. J. Hasham
characters.description: Locked in a Freezer: The resolution of Maggie and PJ'sUSToccurs when they are trapped in a mine collapse, and - thinking they might die - decide to act on their true feelings for one another. My God, What Have I Done?: PJ goes through a period of self doubt after shooting Raylene Darcy.

characters.name: Maggie Doyle
characters.description: Character Death:Shot by her brother. Locked in a Freezer: The resolution of Maggie and PJ'sUSToccurs when they are trapped in a mine collapse, and - thinking they might die - decide to act on their true feelings for one another. The Missus and the Ex: Maggie arrived in Mt. Thomas to be reunited with her academy-mate and ex-boyfriend Wayne, now married to Roz. The Smurfette Principle: Was the only regular female officer for most of the first three seasons. Stereo Fibbing: Inverted in the first episode when Tom introduces Maggie to Wayne. Wayne tells him that they met at the pub last night, at the same time as Maggie tells him they were at the police academy together, and Tom asks, "Well, which is it?" Both are true.

characters.name: Nick Schultz
characters.description: Backstory: Works in traffic because he lost his wife and daughter in a car crash. Brick Joke: Actor WilliamMcInnesrevealed how he would always order catering and one time he had fourteen pork rolls, much to the displeasure of his co stars. Five years after he left the role there was an episode where Ben and his basketball team all fell sick after eating pork rolls, a nod to the amusing incident. Drill Sergeant Nasty: When Nick tried out as sergeant and running the station he was considerably worse than his normal prickly behaviour. Good Is Not Nice: In order to help Dash get over her fear of driving he makes her relive the car crash she was in. To help Tom's daughter in the sway of a cult he roars, yells and screams at her about how his wife was brainwashed into turning on her family. Jack Bauer Interrogation Technique: Nick tries this on a pedophile bragging about how evil he is, and again when he roars at a station bombing suspect.

characters.name: Wayne Patterson
characters.description: Character Death: Run over by a criminal. Stereo Fibbing: Inverted in the first episode when Tom introduces Maggie to Wayne. Wayne tells him that they met at the pub last night, at the same time as Maggie tells him they were at the police academy together, and Tom asks, "Well, which is it?" Both are true, but it's obvious to Tom that Wayne is holding something back.

characters.name: Adam Cooper
characters.description: Back for the Finale: A regular for most of the first five seasons before being fired for corruption, returned in the final two-parter to frame Tom. Sixth Ranger: Adam arrives about two thirds of the way through season 1.

characters.name: DashMcKinley
characters.description: Backstory: Was nearly raped in school and decided to join the police force.

characters.name: Jo Parrish
characters.description: Character Death:Killed in the station bombing.

characters.name: Tess Gallagher
characters.description: Drill Sergeant Nasty: Neither a drill sergeant nor that nasty, but Tess was initially rather officious and very much rubbed people the wrong way.

characters.name: Evan Jones
characters.description: Comically Missing the Point: In Jonesy's first episode there is drag racing and one of the drivers turns out to have a suspected stolen engine in his car. He claims to have been fitted up and Tess, knowing Jones has just about everything in his personal vehicle, asks is he happens to have a file that she thinks might have been used to remove the serial number. Not knowing what Tess is getting at he tells her no, but he does have an angle grinder. Punctuated!  For!  Emphasis!: Evan pulls this at times."You shot''your own'' '''''daughter!!!'''''""What have you done?What haveyou done?"

characters.name: Susie Raynor
characters.description: Femme Fatale: Susie goes from Evan to Ben; who had shot her husband, to Evan again to Alex to a victim of a stolen car, in less than a year.

characters.name: Joss Peroni
characters.description: Cowboy Cop: Joss in shown in his first episode being caught out fantasizing he'sDirty Harry.

characters.name: Mark Jacobs
characters.description: Author Avatar: Mark Jacobs was used to comment on issues such as enforcing road laws for revenue raising or politics (such as how one offender being allowed to vote explains the Liberals, and his amusement when a shooting has a picture of Prime Minister John Howard was shot.)

characters.name: Matt Graham
characters.description: The Stoic: Having joined the police force after the tragedies he witnessed as a soldier in East Timor.

characters.name: Charlie Clarke
characters.description: Alliterative Name:CharlieClarke

characters.name: Nell Croydon
characters.description: The Ghost: Mentioned ofthen but never seen throughout the first season, until she was killed in a car crash towards the end of it.

characters.name: Grace Curtis
characters.description: Killed Off for Real:Killed by the Baxters.

characters.name: Monica Draper
characters.description: Internal Affairs: Usually shows up working for Ethical Standards Department. Inspector Javert: Initially came across this way with PJ, but she was consistently more reasonable than Falcon-Price was with Tom.

characters.name: Russell Falcon-Price
characters.description: Glory Hound: The high brass do get concerned about the police image however. Inspector Javert: Falcon-Price was hell-bent with ending the careers of Ben, Tom, anyone who did not fit his mold and closing the station, to the point wherehe puts Tom up to active duty, knowing he just had cancer surgery and was not up to it.

characters.name: Clancy Freeman
characters.description: Disability Superpower: Clancy was instrumental to solving several cases despite his handicaps, as well as quite likely saving Tom's grandson from brain damage. Killed Off for Real:Killed in the station bombing.

characters.name: Hayley Fulton
characters.description: Pac-Man Fever: Tess adopts Hailey from a broken home and is so determined to give her a good Christmas she thinks to get her aNintendo. They are playingSuper Mario 64later and already up to one of the last levels, so either Tess got it second hand, they are the best video game players ever or someone goofed.

characters.name: Zoe Hamilton
characters.description: Slap-Slap-Kiss: With Nick, eventually leading to their marriage and moving to Richmond.

characters.name: Tony Timms
characters.description: Alliterative Name:TonyTimms

characters.name: Jack Woodley
characters.description: Sixth Ranger Traitor: Wayne's first replacement Jack Woodley, a detective who was shortly afterwards caughtplanting evidence.
